title: AI Proxy Dispatcher Config
description:
Configuration for the AI proxy dispatcher plugin. Exposes a unified OpenAI-compatible API and routes to LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama). Supports named targets for policy-driven routing (via the `cel` middleware), glob-based dynamic model routing (`routes`), provider fallback on 5xx, and token count propagation into context for downstream middlewares.

**Protocol surfaces** (path-based dispatch, ADR-0030 §1):
- `POST /v1/chat/completions` — OpenAI Chat Completions (translated for Anthropic).
- `POST /v1/responses` — OpenAI Responses API, stateless (ADR-0030 §2). Translated for Anthropic. Rejected for Ollama (no upstream Responses surface). `previous_response_id` returns 400; `store: true` is permissive but emits a `Warning: 299` header. The synthetic response `id` is `resp_<uuid-v7>`.
- `GET /v1/models` — model catalog aggregator (ADR-0030 §4). Aggregates from every unique provider declared in `routes` / `targets` / flat. On per-provider failure returns `200 OK` with `partial: true` + a `warnings[]` array.
- Any other path routes through Chat Completions for backward compatibility with operator-defined custom paths.

The model identifier is **always** taken from the client's `model` field on the request body (ADR-0030 §0 — caller-owned model). Gateway config declares **providers** (where to go, with what credentials), never an authoritative model list. A request that omits `model` is rejected with 400 problem+json (`urn:barbacane:error:model_required`).

Three resolution modes (evaluated in order):
- **Context** (`ai.target`): a `cel` middleware writes `ai.target: <name>` into context; the dispatcher uses `targets[<name>]`.
- **Routes**: each entry's glob `pattern` is matched against the client's `model`; first match wins.
- **Default / flat**: `default_target` → `targets[<name>]`, else the top-level `provider` config.

Catalog policy is attached to the **target** via `allow` / `deny` glob lists. They apply on every resolution path that produces a target carrying them — including context-driven dispatch — so a `cel` misconfig cannot leak a denied model. A denied model returns 403 (`urn:barbacane:error:model_not_permitted`) and does not fall through to a different provider.

The `fallback` list is tried in order when the resolved target returns a 5xx or a connection error.

GlobPattern.description: Glob pattern (`*`, `?`, `[...]`). Matched case-sensitively against the client's `model` field. Restricted character set so vacuum surfaces nonsense like regex syntax at lint time; runtime uses the `globset` crate.

Auth.description: Credential attachment strategy. Orthogonal to `provider` (which picks the wire protocol): the provider only supplies a default convention, which this overrides. Lets an OpenAI-compatible endpoint with a non-standard credential header be configured without a new provider — e.g. Brave AI Grounding (`X-Subscription-Token`), Azure OpenAI (`api-key`), or a key-in-query API. When omitted, defaults to `bearer` for OpenAI/Ollama and `api_key` (the `x-api-key` header) for Anthropic.

header.description: Arbitrary credential header name; the key is sent as its value (e.g. `X-Subscription-Token`, `api-key`).

query.description: Query-string parameter name; the key is appended to the upstream URL as `?<param>=<key>` (e.g. Google Gemini's `key`).

TargetConfig.description: A named provider target: provider type, credentials, optional custom endpoint, and optional catalog `allow` / `deny` policy. The model identifier comes from the client request body, never from this config (ADR-0030 §0).

provider.description: LLM provider. `openai` and `ollama` are OpenAI-compatible (passthrough). `anthropic` uses the Messages API with automatic request/response translation.

api_key.description: Provider API key. Use a secret reference (`env://VAR` or `file://...`) rather than a literal value, so the key is resolved at runtime and never baked into the compiled artifact. Omit for Ollama (unauthenticated local endpoint).

base_url.description: Custom provider base URL. Defaults: OpenAI → `https://api.openai.com`, Anthropic → `https://api.anthropic.com`, Ollama → `http://localhost:11434`. Use this for Azure OpenAI, self-hosted vLLM, remote Ollama, etc.

allow.description: Catalog allow-list of glob patterns. When set, the client's `model` must match at least one entry, otherwise 403 `model_not_permitted`. Combine with `deny` if needed (`deny` is evaluated after `allow`).

deny.description: Catalog deny-list of glob patterns. When set, the client's `model` must not match any entry, otherwise 403 `model_not_permitted`. Evaluated after `allow`.

RouteEntry.description: Glob-pattern routing rule: when the client's `model` matches `pattern`, dispatch to the configured `provider`. First match wins; a denied model does not fall through to the next route. Has its own `allow` / `deny` for catalog policy on the route's provider.

pattern.description: Glob pattern matched against the client's `model` (e.g. `claude-*`, `gpt-4o*`, `o[1-4]*`, `*` as a catch-all).

api_key.description: Provider API key. Use a secret reference (`env://VAR` or `file://...`) rather than a literal value, so the key is resolved at runtime and never baked into the compiled artifact.

provider.description: Provider for the flat single-provider config. Required when `targets` is not defined.

api_key.description: API key for the flat config. Use a secret reference (`env://VAR` or `file://...`) rather than a literal value, so the key is resolved at runtime and never baked into the compiled artifact.

base_url.description: Custom base URL for the flat config. Overrides the provider default.

timeout.description: Request timeout in seconds for LLM dispatch (chat completions, responses). LLM calls can take 10–60 s; set higher for long-running completions.

models_timeout_ms.description: Per-provider request timeout in **milliseconds** for the `/v1/models` aggregator (ADR-0030 §4). The aggregator queries every unique provider sequentially; using the LLM `timeout` here would mean one hung upstream blocks the response for `timeout` × number-of-hung-providers seconds. Discovery doesn't need that much patience — the default keeps `/v1/models` responsive even when one provider is degraded.

max_tokens.description: Default `max_tokens` injected into the request when the client omits it. Required for Anthropic (enforced by the API). Acts as a cost guardrail for OpenAI/Ollama.

fallback.description: Ordered list of fallback providers. Tried in sequence when the primary target returns a 5xx or a connection error. 4xx responses are returned directly to the client without fallback.

routes.description: Ordered glob-pattern routing rules evaluated against the client's `model` after `ai.target` context lookup but before `default_target` and the flat config. First match wins; a denied model does not fall through to the next route.

targets.description: Named provider targets for policy-driven routing. The `cel` middleware selects the active target by writing `ai.target` into context before this dispatcher runs. Example: `cel` sets `ai.target: premium` → dispatcher uses the `premium` target config.

default_target.description: Name of the target to use when no `ai.target` context key is present and no route matched. Must match a key in `targets`. When omitted and no context target is set, falls back to the flat `provider` config.
